narration: Buy new sneakers, and suddenly your old bag looks shabby. Then your jacket. Then everything. That cascading urge is called the Diderot Effect.
visual: Orange sneaker SVG springs in from center. Arrow points to old bag with red X overlay. $3,000 counter fades in. Text: ONE PURCHASE TRIGGERS THEM ALL.

narration: Philosopher Denis Diderot got a fancy robe in 1769. Suddenly his study felt wrong — he bought a new desk, then a chair, then everything.
visual: Coat/robe SVG springs in, arrow appears, desk SVG fades in, second arrow, chair SVG fades in. Badge: NAMED AFTER PHILOSOPHER DENIS DIDEROT.

narration: Your brain craves harmony — psychologists call it unity theory. One upgrade breaks that balance, so your brain demands everything else gets upgraded too.
visual: Brain SVG with UNITY THEORY label. Three numbered steps slide in: BUY SOMETHING NEW, OLD STUFF LOOKS WRONG, BUY MORE TO MATCH.

narration: Studies show the average Diderot cascade costs $3,000. Triggered by big purchases — furniture, cars, electronics, or even a single outfit.
visual: Counter animates $0 to $3,000. Four SVG icons spring in sequentially: couch, phone, car, shirt. Title: THE $3,000 SPIRAL.

narration: Seventy-three percent of people feel this after a big purchase. It's not a personal flaw — it's your brain's wiring. And now you can stop it.
visual: Large 73% text fades in. Progress bar fills to 73%. Ten person silhouettes appear — seven orange (affected), three grey. Badge: IT'S BRAIN WIRING.

narration: The fix? One in, one out — every time something new comes in, something old goes out. People who follow it save an average of $2,400 a year.
visual: ONE IN box and ONE OUT box spring in with arrow between. Two checkmarks slide in. Orange badge: SAVES THE AVERAGE PERSON $2,400 PER YEAR.
